title: Multi-Tenant Hospital Management System (HMS)

requirements.text: Implement Internet Identity authentication as the sole login method, replacing JWT. Map roles (Super Admin, Hospital Admin, Doctor, Patient) to principals stored in the backend. Enforce role-based access control so each principal can only access endpoints and data permitted by their role.
acceptanceCriteria: Users can log in via Internet Identity
Each user principal is assigned exactly one role
API calls from a principal not matching the required role are rejected
Passwords are not stored (Internet Identity handles credentials)

requirements.text: Implement multi-tenancy in the backend: every Hospital Admin, Doctor, Supplier, Medicine, Bed, BloodStock, and Appointment record must carry a hospitalId. All queries must be scoped to the caller's hospitalId so data from one hospital is never visible to another hospital.

quotes: Each hospital has its own separate data
Data of one hospital should not be visible to another hospital

acceptanceCriteria: A Hospital Admin from Hospital A cannot retrieve records belonging to Hospital B
All backend query functions filter by the caller's hospitalId
Super Admin can retrieve records across all hospitals

requirements.text: Create backend data models and CRUD operations for: Users, Hospitals, Medicines (with stock quantity, minimum quantity, expiry date, supplierId), Suppliers, Beds (type: general/ICU, status: available/occupied), BloodStock (blood group, quantity, minimum quantity), Appointments (patientId, doctorId, hospitalId, datetime, status), and Reports (aggregated snapshots).

quotes: Create collections/tables: Users, Hospitals, Medicines, Suppliers, Beds, BloodStock, Appointments, Reports

acceptanceCriteria: All eight entity types are persisted in stable backend storage
CRUD operations are exposed for each entity
Medicines store expiry date and minimum stock threshold
Beds distinguish between general and ICU types
BloodStock stores all standard ABO/Rh blood groups

requirements.text: Implement Medicine Management: Hospital Admins can add, update, and delete medicines. Track current stock quantity. Expose a query that returns medicines with stock below minimum quantity (low stock alert). Expose a query that returns medicines whose expiry date has passed or is within 7 days (expiry alert).
acceptanceCriteria: Hospital Admin can create, read, update, delete medicines scoped to their hospital
getLowStockMedicines returns medicines where currentStock < minimumQuantity
getExpiringMedicines returns medicines expiring within 7 days or already expired
Stock quantity updates automatically when a treatment record uses a medicine

requirements.text: Implement Supplier Management: Hospital Admins can add and edit suppliers. Each medicine record can reference a supplierId. Expose a query returning all medicines linked to a given supplier.
acceptanceCriteria: Hospital Admin can create and update supplier records
Medicines can be associated with a supplier
Query returns all medicines for a given supplierId within the caller's hospital

requirements.text: Implement Bed Management: Hospital Admins can add beds with type (General or ICU) and toggle status (Available/Occupied). Expose queries for total beds, available beds, occupied beds, and ICU bed counts per hospital.
acceptanceCriteria: Bed records include type (General/ICU) and status (Available/Occupied)
getBedStats returns total, available, occupied, and ICU counts
Patients can query available beds for their hospital

requirements.text: Implement Blood Bank Management: Hospital Admins can manage blood group inventory (A+, A-, B+, B-, AB+, AB-, O+, O-) with quantity and minimum threshold. Expose a query returning blood groups below minimum quantity.
acceptanceCriteria: All eight standard blood groups are tracked per hospital
getLowBloodStock returns groups where quantity < minimumQuantity
Patients can query blood availability for their hospital

requirements.text: Implement Doctor Management: Hospital Admins can add doctors with specialization and set their availability schedule. Expose queries for doctor list and availability per hospital.
acceptanceCriteria: Doctor records include name, specialization, and availability schedule
Hospital Admin can add and update doctor records
Patients can retrieve the list of available doctors for their hospital

requirements.text: Implement the Appointment System: Patients can book appointments by selecting a doctor and available time slot. Doctors can view their upcoming appointments. Hospital Admins can view all appointments for their hospital.
acceptanceCriteria: Patients can create an appointment record linked to a doctorId and hospitalId
Doctors see only appointments assigned to them
Hospital Admin sees all appointments within their hospital
Appointment status can be updated (scheduled, completed, cancelled)

requirements.text: Implement a Reports module: backend exposes aggregated report queries for medicine usage, current stock, expired medicines, bed occupancy rate, and blood stock levels — all scoped per hospital. Super Admin can request reports across all hospitals.
acceptanceCriteria: getMedicineStockReport returns current quantities and highlights low/expired items
getBedOccupancyReport returns percentage of occupied beds, broken down by type
getBloodStockReport returns current quantities per blood group
Super Admin report aggregates data across all hospitals

requirements.text: Build a Super Admin dashboard page showing: total number of hospitals, list of hospitals with status, and system-wide report summaries. Super Admin can create new hospitals and assign Hospital Admin principals to them.
acceptanceCriteria: Super Admin sees a stats card row: total hospitals, total users
Table lists all hospitals with name, admin, and status
Form to create a new hospital and assign a Hospital Admin principal
System-wide report summary cards are displayed

requirements.text: Build a Hospital Admin dashboard page with sidebar navigation linking to: Medicines, Suppliers, Beds, Blood Bank, Doctors, Appointments, Staff, and Reports sections. Dashboard home shows summary stat cards for each module.
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar with links to all Hospital Admin modules is present
Dashboard home shows cards: total medicines, low stock count, bed occupancy, blood stock alerts, pending appointments
Each module page renders a searchable, filterable data table
Add/edit forms are accessible from each module page

requirements.text: Build a Doctor dashboard page showing: list of assigned patients with appointment details, a form to update patient treatment notes and deduct medicine usage, and a read-only medicine availability view.
acceptanceCriteria: Doctor sees only their own appointments
Doctor can update appointment status and add treatment notes
Medicine availability panel shows current stock for the hospital
Treatment update automatically triggers stock deduction in the backend

requirements.text: Build a Patient dashboard page allowing patients to: book appointments by browsing doctors and available slots, view their own appointments, check bed availability, check blood group availability, and view their prescribed medicines.
acceptanceCriteria: Doctor availability calendar or slot picker is shown when booking
Patient can see their upcoming and past appointments
Bed availability summary card is shown
Blood group availability table is shown
Prescribed medicines list is visible per appointment

requirements.text: Implement in-app alert notifications: display a notification badge/panel showing low stock medicine alerts, expiring medicines (within 7 days), low blood stock alerts, and any other system warnings. Alerts are scoped to the logged-in user's hospital.
acceptanceCriteria: Notification bell icon in the top navbar shows unread alert count
Dropdown panel lists each alert with type, message, and timestamp
Alerts refresh when the user navigates between pages
Alerts are only shown for the current user's hospital

requirements.text: Implement dark mode toggle: users can switch between light and dark themes. The preference is persisted in localStorage and applied on page load.
acceptanceCriteria: Toggle button is visible in the top navbar
All pages and components render correctly in both light and dark mode
Theme preference is stored in localStorage and restored on reload

requirements.text: Implement search and filter controls on all data tables (Medicines, Suppliers, Beds, Blood Bank, Doctors, Appointments). Search is client-side for the currently loaded page data.
acceptanceCriteria: Each data table has a search input that filters rows by relevant text fields
Medicines table can be filtered by stock status (low stock, expired, normal)
Appointments table can be filtered by status
Filters reset when navigating away and back

requirements.text: Implement client-side PDF export for reports: on each report page, a button triggers a PDF download of the currently displayed report data using a frontend PDF library.
acceptanceCriteria: Each report page has an 'Export to PDF' button
Clicking the button downloads a PDF containing the report title, date, and tabular data
PDF export works for Medicine Stock, Bed Occupancy, and Blood Stock reports

requirements.text: Apply a clean, modern medical-grade visual theme: use a white and teal/green primary palette with crisp sans-serif typography, card-based layouts with subtle shadows, a collapsible sidebar, and consistent status-color coding (red for alerts, green for available, amber for warnings). Enforce this theme consistently across all pages and both light/dark modes.
acceptanceCriteria: Primary color is teal/green; alert accents use red and amber
Sidebar is collapsible and highlights the active route
Dashboard stat cards use icons, bold numbers, and color-coded borders
Layout is responsive and usable on tablet and desktop viewports

requirements.text: Generate a hospital dashboard logo/icon and a hero illustration for the login/landing page.
acceptanceCriteria: Logo icon is rendered in the sidebar header
Hero illustration is displayed on the login page background or panel

constraints: Backend must use a single Motoko actor (no microservices or multiple canisters).
Authentication must use Internet Identity only — no JWT, no external auth providers.
No external databases (MongoDB, PostgreSQL, etc.) — use Motoko stable storage.
No Node.js/Express backend — all backend logic in Motoko.
No email notification service integrations (SendGrid, SMTP, etc.).
Frontend immutable paths (hooks/useInternetIdentity, hooks/useActor, main.tsx, components/ui) must not be modified.
nonGoals: MongoDB or any external database integration
Email notifications via SMTP or third-party services

required: Minimalist teal and white hospital cross logo icon on a clean rounded square background (hms-logo-icon.dim_128x128.png)
Modern medical dashboard hero illustration showing abstract hospital building silhouette with teal gradient and subtle data visualization elements (hms-hero-illustration.dim_1200x600.png)
